body:
Consider this case: 

1. in an issue, we launch orchestrate, which launches implement and creates a PR ; then the orchestrate stops
2. after that, in the PR, we launch orchestrate again -> it seems the new orchestrate route does not share the same context as the first orchestrate? 

@sepo-agent /answer can you confirm this? here is an example that you can directly look into https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/56#issuecomment-4349994663

comments.body:
Yes, confirmed.

The current behavior is: a new manual `/orchestrate` mention starts a new orchestration route from the current target state. It does **not** continue the prior issue-originated orchestrate session/context.

Evidence:

- The router always dispatches a manual `/orchestrate` with `AUTOMATION_CURRENT_ROUND: "1"` and `SOURCE_ACTION: orchestrate`:  
  https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L653-L665
- In `orchestrate-handoff.ts`, `SOURCE_ACTION=orchestrate` takes the manual-start path, not the action-originated continuation path:  
  https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L257-L268
- Manual PR starts are deterministic: if the PR is open and not `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, it dispatches `review`:  
  https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L202-L224
- Docs also say manual `/orchestrate` starts use deterministic status checks, and planner/session continuation is separate for orchestrator/action-originated handoffs:  
  https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L67-L84

For the concrete example, the comment on PR #56:

> `@sepo-agent /orchestrate firstly fix merge conflicts then launch a review agent`

was treated as a fresh manual PR orchestration. Since the PR did not have `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, the manual PR heuristic dispatched `review` first. That matches the bot comment:

> `manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review`

So yes: the second `/orchestrate` on the PR did **not** share the first issue `/orchestrate` chain context. It only used current PR state plus the new request text. After that new PR-originated chain started, its own launched workflows carried `orchestration_enabled: true`, so that second chain could continue from `review -> fix-pr -> review`.

Current limitation: there is no explicit cross-surface “orchestration chain id” or issue-to-PR session continuation for manual `/orchestrate` restarts. To support the behavior you expected, we’d need to carry an origin target / chain identity from issue orchestrate into the PR, then have later PR `/orchestrate` discover and resume or fork from that chain instead of starting at round 1 from PR state.

title: Question: session continuation for the orchestrate route
